com.mentor.chs.api.query

Interface IXExtendedReportProcessor

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXConnectivityProcessor, IXPlugin, IXReportProcessor

    public interface IXExtendedReportProcessor
extends IXReportProcessor
    /** Report processors decide how entities queried for in reports are related to those returned by the scoping queries. A custom processsor for report generation can implement this interface to define how query results from scoping queries are related to entities queried for in reports. To make performmance improvements the report processor has to realize the execution of all the queried entities on a design simultanously. Once the design is loaded all the entities required should be fetched before moving onto scanning the next design. The set of queries to be executed are all passed to the processor and are exected as a single unit per design.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.util.Map<IXExtendedQuery,java.util.Collection<IXObject>> getRelatedObjects(java.util.Collection<IXExtendedQuery> queries, java.util.Collection<IXObject> inputSet) 

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.api.query.IXReportProcessor

        getName, getRelatedObjects, setUsePlugin

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.api.query.IXConnectivityProcessor

        flushCache

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXPlugin

        getDescription, getVersion

    • Method Detail

      • getRelatedObjects

        java.util.Map<IXExtendedQuery,java.util.Collection<IXObject>> getRelatedObjects(java.util.Collection<IXExtendedQuery> queries,
                                                                                java.util.Collection<IXObject> inputSet)
        Parameters:
        queries - the set of queries written in CER and embedded reports. The set of queries can be of a particular report module or could be from multiple report modules of the report. For example if the filtering page is used in CER then all the queries in all the report modules(nested modules as well) will be used. All these queries will be passed in the queries argument to determine the result of executing the query on the whole scope. For the case where the report is not nested the results obtained are the final results. For the case of nested report in addition to the above, the outer query results are used one after the other (for example if the outer query is a design query, then each design results is used) to find all the related entities in a particular report module. For example in case of harness component BOM if the filtering is for a connector with name = Conn.*. Note that in this report outer query is the design query, inner queries are 16 for component types. Then the ReportProcessor calls can be understood in the following steps. It will be called for getting the designs, connectors, wires etc.., related to the filter. (Note that here the queries of the outer and inner report module are used, in a way read it as all queries in all nesting depths). The input set at this stage is all connectors spread across all the designs and satisfying the filter in the CER filter page. Starting from the second call for each design the queries argument will have connector, wires etc.., of the particular inner report module executed over a single result of the outer Query. (Note that there can be multiple outer Query results and the inner report module is called for each outer Q result). In the current example the inputset to the second call is a single design passed one after the other per call to the processor.
        inputSet - collection of extensibility objects whose related objects are to be fetched. These inputSet are obtained by executing the scoping queries on the project selected. For example if the build list is selected and the wire list report is desired then the inputset is the collection of all the designs in that build list. Alternatively if the filtering page is used. The input set will be only those entities in the selected designs which satify the filtering condition. If the report has nested Queries then the inputset for the inner report module is the result of the outer report module query.
        Returns:
        the map of results per query. The query is executed on the input set and the result is grouped by query being executed.
